subject: Product Management
title: Speed Kills (When It Replaces Thinking)
description: One-minute skill: Speed Kills (When It Replaces Thinking).

hook.text: Samsung rushed the Note 7 by two weeks and lost $5.3 billion. Speed without safety is the most expensive kind of fast.

buildup.text: Silicon Valley glorifies speed: 'Move fast and break things.' But some things — like batteries and airplanes — shouldn't break.

discovery.text: Facebook itself retired its famous motto. After years of data scandals, 'move fast and break things' aged poorly.

twist.text: Speed is valuable when it means faster learning cycles. It's dangerous when it means skipping safety, testing, or validation.

climax.text: The lesson: move fast in discovery (cheap experiments). Move carefully in delivery (real products with real consequences).

punchline.text: Move fast to learn. Move carefully to build.

quiz.question: When does speed become dangerous in product development?
options: When it means skipping safety, testing, or validation — not just faster learning
Speed is always dangerous in product development
Speed is always beneficial regardless of context
